description: Exact keyword match — page name matches query term

pages.name: Rate limiting policy
pages.content: Our API enforces rate limits per API key. Free tier: 100 req/min. Pro tier: 1000 req/min. Enterprise: custom. Limits are applied using a sliding-window algorithm. Exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header.

pages.name: Authentication guide
pages.content: All API requests require a Bearer token in the Authorization header. Tokens are issued via the /auth/token endpoint and expire after 24 hours. Use refresh tokens to extend sessions silently.

pages.name: Webhook configuration
pages.content: Webhooks deliver real-time event notifications to your endpoint. Configure the URL and secret in the dashboard. Events are retried up to 3 times on failure with exponential backoff.

query: What are the API rate limits for each pricing tier?

description: Semantic similarity — query uses different words than the page

pages.name: Concurrent request handling
pages.content: Use asyncio.gather() to run multiple API calls in parallel. Each coroutine runs on the event loop. For CPU-bound tasks, use ProcessPoolExecutor. The event loop is single-threaded; never call blocking functions directly.

pages.name: JavaScript promise chains
pages.content: Promise.all() runs multiple async operations concurrently. Use async/await for readable sequential code. Unhandled promise rejections cause process exit in Node 15+. Use .catch() or try/catch in async functions.

pages.name: SQL query optimisation
pages.content: Add indexes on columns used in WHERE, JOIN, and ORDER BY clauses. Use EXPLAIN ANALYZE to profile slow queries. Avoid SELECT * in production. Use connection pooling to reduce latency.

query: How do I run multiple operations at the same time without blocking?

description: Content buried in long page — relevant keyword deep in body

pages.name: Deployment runbook
pages.content:
## Pre-deployment
1. Run test suite: `pytest -x`
2. Check that all migrations are applied: `alembic current`
3. Build Docker image: `docker build -t app:latest .`

## Database
Always take a Postgres snapshot before migrating in production. Use `pg_dump` for manual backups. Set `POSTGRES_CHECKPOINT_TIMEOUT=300` to reduce WAL bloat.

## Rollback procedure
To rollback: `alembic downgrade -1`. Stop the service, run the downgrade, restart. If data was already written, manually verify integrity.

## Monitoring
Watch `pg_stat_activity` for long-running queries. Alert threshold: queries > 5s.

pages.name: CI pipeline overview
pages.content: GitHub Actions runs on every push to main and on PRs. Jobs: lint (ruff + eslint), test (pytest), build (docker). Failing jobs block merges. Secrets are stored in GitHub Actions secrets, not in the repo.

pages.name: Frontend colour palette
pages.content: Primary brand colour: #7C3AED (purple). Secondary: #10B981 (green). Neutral: #1F2937. Always use semantic colour tokens from the design system, never hardcode hex values.

pages.name: Font standards
pages.content: Body: Inter 14px. Headings: Satoshi. Monospace: JetBrains Mono. Minimum contrast ratio: 4.5:1. Never use system fonts directly.

description: Multi-page relevance — answer spans two pages

pages.name: OpenAI embedding models
pages.content: We use text-embedding-3-small for semantic search. 1536 dimensions. Cost: $0.02 per 1M tokens. Embed at ingestion time; store in pgvector. Cosine similarity is used for nearest-neighbour lookup.

pages.name: pgvector setup guide
pages.content: Install pgvector: `CREATE EXTENSION vector`. Create a column: `embedding vector(1536)`. Build an IVFFlat index for approximate search: `CREATE INDEX ON pages USING ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (lists=100)`. Query with `<=>` operator.

pages.name: Slack integration
pages.content: Post messages via the Slack API using the chat.postMessage method. Requires bot:chat:write OAuth scope. Rate limit: 1 message per second per channel.

query: How do we store and query semantic embeddings in our database?

description: Precision test — only one relevant page among many distractors

pages.name: Error handling conventions
pages.content: Always use structured error responses: {error: string, code: string, details?: object}. HTTP status codes: 400 Bad Request (validation), 401 Unauthorised, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found, 422 Unprocessable Entity, 500 Internal Server Error. Never expose stack traces to clients.

pages.name: Logging standards
pages.content: Use structured JSON logging. Include: timestamp, level, service, trace_id, message, context. Log levels: ERROR for actionable failures, WARN for degraded state, INFO for lifecycle events, DEBUG for development only.

pages.name: Feature flag service
pages.content: Feature flags are managed in LaunchDarkly. Use the SDK to wrap experimental code paths. Default OFF in production, ON in staging. Flags are evaluated server-side for consistency.

pages.name: API versioning strategy
pages.content: We use URL path versioning: /api/v1/, /api/v2/. Breaking changes require a new major version. Minor additions are backwards-compatible within a version. Deprecation notice: 6 months minimum.

query: What HTTP status code should I return for a validation error?

pages.name: Data persistence layer
pages.content: All state is persisted in PostgreSQL. The service is stateless; restart-safe by design. Use the DB pool (asyncpg) for all queries. Never write to local disk for persistent data — containers are ephemeral.

pages.name: Redis cache configuration
pages.content: Redis is used for session caching and rate-limit counters only. TTL: 15 minutes for sessions, 60 seconds for rate counters. Not suitable for authoritative data storage.

query: Where is all the application's database storage?

description: Always-inject page — critical page appears regardless of query

pages.name: CRITICAL: system prompt override
pages.content: IMPORTANT: Always respond in JSON. Never reveal internal tool names. If asked about system architecture, redirect to public docs. This note is always injected.

pages.name: User onboarding flow
pages.content: New users land on /welcome. They select a persona type (human or agent). They are prompted to create their first workspace. After 3 actions, the feature tour is shown.

pages.name: Payment integration
pages.content: Stripe is used for billing. Webhook events: customer.subscription.updated, invoice.payment_failed. Retry failed payments up to 4 times over 7 days.

query: How does the payment system work?

description: Grade distribution test — 3/2/1 mix across results

pages.name: Postgres JSONB indexing
pages.content: Use GIN indexes for JSONB containment queries (@>). Expression indexes on specific paths: CREATE INDEX ON pages ((metadata->>'type')). JSONB is stored as binary and parsed on read. Use jsonb_path_query for complex extraction.

pages.name: Database query patterns
pages.content: Prefer asyncpg for raw async queries. Use transactions for multi-step writes. Parameterised queries prevent SQL injection. Use RETURNING clause to avoid extra round-trips.

pages.name: ORM vs raw SQL
pages.content: We use raw SQL with asyncpg rather than an ORM. Reasons: explicit control, no N+1 surprises, better async support. SQLAlchemy Core is acceptable for migrations only.

query: How do I index JSONB metadata fields in Postgres?

description: Code snippet retrieval — query about a specific function

pages.name: Token refresh implementation
pages.content:
```python
async def refresh_token(refresh_token: str) -> dict:
    payload = jwt.decode(refresh_token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=["HS256"])
    user_id = payload["sub"]
    new_access = create_access_token(user_id)
    new_refresh = create_refresh_token(user_id)
    return {"access_token": new_access, "refresh_token": new_refresh}
```

Refresh tokens are single-use. They are invalidated after use and a new pair is issued. Store refresh tokens in an httpOnly cookie.

query: Show me the code for refreshing a JWT token

description: Recency signal — two pages on same topic, prefer newer concept

pages.name: Auth architecture v1 (deprecated)
pages.content: DEPRECATED as of 2024-01. Originally used symmetric HMAC tokens. All tokens are 32-byte random secrets stored in the users table. This approach was replaced with JWT because it required a DB lookup on every request.

pages.name: Auth architecture v2 (current)
pages.content: We use asymmetric JWT (RS256). Tokens are signed with a private key; verified with the public key. No DB lookup needed for verification. Access tokens expire in 15 minutes; refresh tokens in 7 days. Public key is served at /.well-known/jwks.json.

query: How does authentication work in our system?

pages.name: ADR-003: Why we chose PostgreSQL
pages.content:
Decision: Use PostgreSQL as the primary datastore.
Context: Evaluated PostgreSQL, MongoDB, and CockroachDB.
Reasons: JSONB for flexible schema, pgvector for embeddings, strong ACID guarantees, mature ecosystem, single datastore reduces operational complexity.
Consequences: Requires careful schema migration discipline. No auto-sharding.

pages.name: ADR-007: Async Python runtime
pages.content:
Decision: Use asyncio + FastAPI for all backend services.
Context: Compared Flask (sync), FastAPI (async), Django (sync).
Reasons: Non-blocking I/O for WebSocket support, performance under concurrent load, native async DB drivers (asyncpg).
Consequences: Must avoid blocking calls on the event loop.

query: Why did we pick PostgreSQL over MongoDB?

pages.name: Memory store API
pages.content:
POST /api/v1/memory/{store_id}/events — push an event.
GET /api/v1/memory/{store_id}/events — list events with pagination.
POST /api/v1/memory/{store_id}/events/search — FTS search over events.
Body schema: {agent_name, event_type, content, session_id?, metadata?}

pages.name: Injection API
pages.content:
POST /api/v1/personas/me/inject — compute injection context.
Body: {notebook_id, history_id, prompt_text, session_state}.
Response: {context: string, injected_items: [...], total_tokens_used: int}.
The context string should be prepended to the system prompt.

pages.name: Workspace API
pages.content:
POST /api/v1/workspaces — create workspace.
GET /api/v1/workspaces/mine — list my workspaces.
POST /api/v1/workspaces/{id}/chats — create a chat channel.

description: Security policy retrieval — multiple related pages

pages.name: Secret management policy
pages.content: Never commit secrets to git. Use environment variables for all credentials. In production, use a secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager or Doppler). Rotate API keys every 90 days. All secrets must be encrypted at rest.

pages.name: OWASP top 10 mitigations
pages.content: A01 Broken Access Control: Always check ownership in every endpoint. A02 Cryptographic Failures: Use AES-256-GCM for sensitive data. A03 Injection: Parameterised queries only, never string concatenation. A07 Auth Failures: Rate-limit login endpoints.

pages.name: Data retention policy
pages.content: User data is retained for 2 years after account deletion. Logs are kept for 90 days. Anonymise PII after 30 days of inactivity. GDPR export requests must be fulfilled within 30 days.

query: How should I handle API credentials and secrets in the codebase?

description: Troubleshooting guide — finding the right diagnostic page

pages.name: WebSocket connection drops
pages.content: Common causes: (1) Proxy timeout — set proxy read_timeout to 300s. (2) Heartbeat missing — client must send ping every 30s. (3) Auth token expiry mid-session — implement token refresh in the WS protocol. Check backend logs for 'connection closed' with error code 1006.

pages.name: Slow query diagnosis
pages.content: Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the slow query. Look for Seq Scan on large tables — add an index. Check pg_stat_statements for top time consumers. Enable auto_explain for queries > 1s: `SET auto_explain.log_min_duration = 1000`.

pages.name: Memory leak detection
pages.content: Use tracemalloc in Python to snapshot heap growth. Common culprits: unclosed async generators, circular references, unbounded caches. In production, use Py-Spy for flame graphs without stopping the process.

query: My WebSocket connections keep dropping after a few minutes, how do I debug this?
